response.justification: Bogdan has exceptional software engineering seniority (23+ years) and proven performance optimization expertise in high-throughput, low-latency systems—directly applicable to workload analysis. However, the resume lacks core hardware architecture experience: no SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture work, or deep learning infrastructure optimization at the level required for a Principal role. His current AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq is encouraging, but hardware-software co-design and DNN workload specifics remain significant gaps. He could succeed with substantial ramp-up in GPU/accelerator architectures, but this represents a notable career pivot from full-stack software development.
strengths: 23+ years of professional experience with demonstrated expertise in real-time, high-performance systems and low-latency optimization (Dunnhumby micro-services, async I/O, fiber-based concurrency)
System-level technical depth: C/C++ background (National Instruments), embedded systems (robotic arms), and proven ability to architect complex systems across multiple domains
Currently engaged in AI infrastructure at re:cinq, showing recent exposure to AI systems and agent frameworks; strong learning trajectory across diverse technology stacks
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling experience—a core responsibility and required skill for this Principal-level role; entirely absent from resume
Career is software-centric with no documented GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD analysis, or deep learning workload optimization experience—all critical for hardware accelerator performance architecture
Limited evidence of hardware-software co-design or export-control/security clearance readiness; no indication of background in physics, electrical engineering, or hardware design disciplines

key_factors.factor: Strong performance optimization and system-level experience
Deep learning workload analysis experience missing
